concepts.title: Opportunity Cost
concepts.description: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative that is given up when a choice is made. It is an important concept in microeconomics as it helps in understanding the trade-offs involved in decision making.

concepts.title: Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility (DMU)
concepts.description: The law of diminishing marginal utility states that as the consumption of a good increases, the marginal utility derived from each additional unit of the good decreases. This law is a fundamental concept in microeconomics and explains why people tend to consume less of a good as its price increases.

concepts.title: Scarcity and Choice
concepts.description: Scarcity is a fundamental problem that arises in microeconomics due to the limited availability of resources. As a result, individuals and societies are forced to make choices about how to allocate their resources, leading to trade-offs and opportunity costs.

concepts.title: Pareto Efficiency
concepts.description: Pareto efficiency is a state of the economy where no individual can be made better off without making someone else worse off. You can remember this concept using the phrase 'Pareto = Perfect Efficiency'.

concepts.title: Misunderstanding of Diminishing Marginal Utility
concepts.description: Many students tend to confuse diminishing marginal utility with decreasing total utility. Diminishing marginal utility refers to the decrease in marginal utility per unit of consumption, whereas decreasing total utility refers to the overall decrease in utility as consumption increases. Students should be careful to distinguish between these two concepts.

concepts.title: Economic Systems - Command Economy, Market Economy, Mixed Economy
concepts.description: Economic systems refer to the way in which resources are allocated in an economy. Command economy, market economy, and mixed economy are the three main types of economic systems. Students should be familiar with the characteristics and features of each type of economic system.

concepts.title: Law of Increasing Opportunity Cost
concepts.description: The law of increasing opportunity cost states that as the production of a good increases, the opportunity cost of producing one more unit of the good also increases. This law is a fundamental concept in microeconomics and explains why the production of a good may eventually become unprofitable.

concepts.title: Supply and Demand
concepts.description: Supply and demand are the two main forces that determine the price of a good in a market economy. Supply refers to the quantity of a good that producers are willing to sell at a given price, while demand refers to the quantity of a good that consumers are willing to buy at a given price.
